mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate declines by more than 25 basis points over a 3-month window, the reduction in short-term borrowing costs triggers a compression of equity risk premiums and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.91% as of 2018-08-15 suggests room for further easing
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns in easing-triggered periods are not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in total nonfarm payroll employment of more than 150,000 jobs per month over a 3-month rolling average signals robust labor market momentum and consumer spending capacity, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during periods of sustained payroll growth exceed baseline by less than 50 basis points on average.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) decline by more than 2% month-over-month, consumer demand weakness signals deteriorating earnings growth expectations, causing S&P 500 to produce negative returns over the next 20 trading days as equity valuations compress.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while remaining positive, indicating supply-side constraints and pricing power that support corporate margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth-inflation tradeoff.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in the specified trigger regime do not exceed baseline by at least 25 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) decline while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously by more than 0.2pp over 2 months, the joint signal indicates demand destruction across both residential and labor markets, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as recession risk premia increase.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over preceding 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,168 thousand and UNRATE at 3.9% as of 2018-07-15
regime_hint: early-stage demand contraction or recession onset
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 60% of instances where both trigger conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.5%, the real interest rate becomes negative, signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations through lower real discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 251.846 and FEDFUNDS at 1.91% as of 2018-08-15
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in the specified regime do not exceed baseline by at least 75 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 4.2% while nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) decelerates to below 100,000 jobs per month, the joint deterioration signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 4.2% AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 100,000 jobs
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% and PAYEMS at 149,279 thousand as of 2018-08-15
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances where both trigger conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized rate while retail sales (RSAFS) remain flat or decline, signaling demand-side weakness that compresses equity earnings multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations reset lower.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2% AND RSAFS month-over-month change < 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18,514.595 billion and RSAFS at 509,025 million as of 2018-04-15 and 2018-08-15 respectively
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in the specified regime do not produce negative returns in at least 58% of triggered instances.
